name: DRY / KISS / YAGNI
def: Don’t Repeat Yourself, Keep It Simple, You Aren’t Gonna Need It — three pragmatic maxims.
problem: Counter the opposite failure modes: duplication, over-complexity, and speculative features.
mechanism: DRY: single source of truth for each piece of knowledge. KISS: prefer the simplest design that works. YAGNI: build only what is needed now.
limits: DRY can over-couple unrelated code that merely looks alike
KISS/YAGNI can under-build for known futures
failures: Forcing DRY on coincidental duplication → wrong abstraction
YAGNI used to dodge necessary design
whenNot: When a known, near-certain requirement justifies upfront design
myths: "DRY means never duplicate anything" — duplicate knowledge is the enemy, not duplicate text
